reviews.author: Yogi Yoga
reviews.text: Got there early.. we were likely the first poking around... the local farmers were friendly and directed us to the ancient well (a square one). We got to be observers in their morning routine... watering, tending to the soil, harvesting... It is a good place to gain an understanding of how this organic farm functions, with multiple farming families involved and the sustainability behind it.

reviews.author: Kanna S.
reviews.text: A charming and peaceful village. It's famous for fresh herbs and organic farming. It’s a lovely place to walk around, experience traditional gardening, and enjoy the relaxing countryside atmosphere. There are also cooking classes available, where you can learn to prepare local Vietnamese dishes using fresh ingredients from the garden.

reviews.author: Pied Piper
reviews.text: I loved the place. I was walking by the paddies and saw huge groups of white people going into an alley. I then read the sign and it was on my to see list. I loved all the salads, herbs and papaya growing all around. I love food and growing stuffs so knew what everything was. Beautiful place that I saw for free. I walked in to look, then after 15 minutes I was done. So pretty

reviews.author: Wayne Ireland
reviews.text: We had such a great time at Tra Que Vegetable Village! The vegetable tour was a wonderful hands-on experience for our kids — they loved learning how the farmers till the soil, plant crops, and water the fields. It really gave them an appreciation of the hard work that goes into food. After the tour, we joined a cooking class and enjoyed a delicious three-course meal for lunch. The staff were friendly, helpful, and made the whole experience easy and fun. To top it off, we even received a relaxing foot and shoulder massage, which was such a thoughtful touch after the farm work! Highly recommend this experience for families or anyone looking to learn, taste, and relax all in one place.

reviews.author: Cam Lau
reviews.text: Highly recommend this tour. Kids stayed engaged and did some hard work on the tools. Cooking class after was really fun. Lunch just delicious. 10/10.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Tra Que Vegetable Village is just a pleasant walk from An Bang Beach and offers a peaceful escape surrounded by greenery. The rows of herbs and vegetables are beautifully maintained, and there are a few cozy cafes and restaurants around the edges using the fresh produce in their dishes, it’s a lovely setting for a slow wander. When we visited, none of the activities like farming tours or cooking classes were taking place, which was a bit of a letdown since they’re often featured in brochures and signs. It would’ve been helpful to know in advance, especially if you’re coming for the hands-on experience. That said, it’s still a nice spot to explore if you’re after a quieter moment just outside the main town.

reviews.author: Jitka Prochazkova
reviews.text: Today I experienced an amazing day at this cooking school in a rural village, where meals are prepared using local ingredients with a wonderful guide named Vi ❤️. She picked me up at my hotel in the morning, and we arrived at the local market by scooter, where she explained the basic ingredients needed for cooking as well as other special products sold there. She also bought me some types of fruit that I didn’t know before and wanted to try. After that, we moved to the place where they live and where the cooking takes place. I received a welcome drink, and then we went for a walk through the fields where they grow vegetables and herbs. Afterwards, I was given a chef’s hat and we started cooking a four-course menu. Everyone there, including Vi, was incredibly kind and friendly. At the end of the cooking class, I received a certificate and a small gift as a souvenir of this beautiful day. The four-course menu was absolutely delicious, and I will definitely enjoy cooking it again at home. I highly recommend this experience to everyone. It is not a mass tourism activity, and I recommend booking it directly through the Tra Que Cooking School website. It was truly a beautiful experience for me.

reviews.author: Do Cuong
reviews.text: Located right between Hoi An’s Old Town and An Bang beach, Tra Que village is a really unique spot to visit. It’s famous for its amazing veggies and herbs, and it's been around for centuries. The farmers here use these super unique, totally chemical-free methods to grow their herbs. That’s why you’ll find the village's fresh products in some of Hoi An's best dishes, like Cao Lau.

reviews.author: Cam Prince
reviews.text: What an incredible experience! It was fascinating to observe how locals in Vietnam cultivate and purchase their produce. I highly recommend visiting with a small group (on bikes) and a knowledgeable guide who can explain the diverse crops being grown. You’ll even have the opportunity to try traditional farming methods, like watering and turning over the soil yourself.

reviews.author: GAN LAY YONG
reviews.text: Visiting the place contributes to the preservation of traditional occupancy village. We saw how to plough the land before adding compost, then planting the green vegetables. Also saw how watering of the vegetables is done. Saw a lot of green vegetables, papaya, winter melons. Really an eye opener

reviews.author: Peter B
reviews.text: I arrived at 8am and there were no other tourists, just workers. Get here earlier if you want to catch the golden morning light. I asked permission to take people’s photos and 85% were fine with it (1 or 2 even posed)! It’s pleasant to stroll around and see the different vegetables and tools that are used. Just avoid the ‘slow cafe’ if you want a drink afterwards, overpriced and not good. I wish I’d tried one of the other spots next to the gardens.

reviews.author: Ninon M. (Globedwellers)
reviews.text: We didn’t visit the garden itself per se, but we explored and rode around the village on our electric scooters—and it’s truly a lovely place to discover. The local community is very friendly, and the gardens are beautifully maintained. This is where the 100% organic, delicious vegetables that supply many of Hoi An’s best restaurants are grown. As French natives who love growing our own vegetables, we deeply appreciate fresh, local produce and are very thankful for the hard work and dedication of these talented gardeners. During our one-month stay, we thoroughly enjoyed their beautiful harvests. We were especially impressed by how precisely and skilfully everything is done—traditionally, as it should be. It really is an art, and it deserves recognition. Next time we visit, we’ll make sure to stop for a meal on the island and learn more about this incredible gardening community.

reviews.author: Alnoor
reviews.text: This is another place where is a good suggestion for couple of hours walking around and taking a cooking class. Various options. Best place for coffee is slow cafe towards the street side. It is a Japanese style house complete with koi fish. Coffee is excellent.

reviews.author: Jonathan Okeke
reviews.text: 100% organically grown vegetables. A tourist haven for sustainable farming. Offers classes in preparing Vietnamese cuisine. NB: If you cycle, you can treat yourself to cycling around the farm village and onto the beautiful sites in the nearby town.

reviews.author: Quezon 7
reviews.text: Did not see any of these during my morning walk to this place, maybe you have to book in advance. Call 1st before visiting to be sure they are open and can accomodate you.

reviews.author: Evrim NUMANOGLU OZGEN
reviews.text: It is a peaceful place to be. However there is nothing special unless you have never seen a vegetable garden before There are some very nice cafes to have food and drinks. But the overall atmosphere is very touristy to the extend of silliness. That people would pay to water the plants and call this a local experience in Vietnam!!! It was sad to see how disconnected some visitors were from nature during their day to day lives.

reviews.author: Andrew Yeoh
reviews.text: This is another way for the locals to improve their livelihood by making full use of their natural surroundings. Tourists are given an opportunity to try their hand in a little bit of basic farming. After that, they are given an opportunity to soak their feet in special herbs. Finally, they get to try to cook a simple Vietnamese dish. We had our lunch there as well.

reviews.author: Mr. RaTha
reviews.text: Now they did not grow to much!!! Inside the village has many homestay for tourists who want to become a farmer and live with farmer so you can choose this place to experience. Not need ticket when you come to visit the village. They have e-car to take tourist go to inside the village from the Hightway or from your hotel in Hoi An town ( 5Km far way) And you can collect some local dishes in the village coz it has many restaurants for tourists. Best time to visit: in the morining 8am to 11am you can see many activities from the farmer and in the afternoon you can spend your time to visit it from 3pm to 5pm because it is less sunny and windy now. When you go there please carry with your: sunglasses, suncreams, bottle of waters... Recommend....

reviews.author: An Hoe
reviews.text: A small village suitable for cycling around. Homestay, cooking class, cafes are all available. Greenery vibes which is so nice. The most favorite part of Hoi An that shouldn't be missed!

reviews.author: Share Share
reviews.text: Good place to view and enjoy yhe vegetable farming. The farmers welcome us and introduce some of the vegetables and herbs. It was early morning, the farmers started their watering routine.

reviews.author: Melody Sol
reviews.text: Discover the joy of being in nature at Tra Que Vegetable Village🌿Dive into hands-on gardening, pick fresh produce, and enjoy the simple pleasures of the earth. Join our Cooking Class to turn your harvest into delicious Vietnamese dishes. Discover new places, appreciate cultural richness and make a meaningful impact wherever you venture 🙏 #summer #mytiawellness #wellnessresort #tiawellnessresort #healthy #nature #travel #tour #traque #hoian

reviews.author: V LS
reviews.text: Such an amazing place perfect for kids and families to explore and learn about planting

reviews.author: Nir Atar
reviews.text: Nice community garden but not worth the detour or the 35,000 vnd payment imho

reviews.author: Clo
reviews.text: So pretty I really loved watching the local people working on the beautiful gardens in a traditional way, it was lovely and peaceful too. There’s a lovely cafe nearby facing it :)

reviews.author: Thomas Amos
reviews.text: Cool gardens with great cafe around the outside. Worth a visit

reviews.author: Glenda Young (Princess In Transit)
reviews.text: Halfway between the beach & Ancient Town, Tra Que is very busy with visitors on pushbike tours, cooking schools & cafes. But it’s cool & quiet & very pretty so I guess that’s understandable.

reviews.author: Arsala Khan
reviews.text: Its situated between the old city, river and the beach. Vast fields grown with vegetables and herbs, great place to come early in morning or near sunset to enjoy a jog or cycling along the green fields. There are many resturants in this area as well. So you can enjoy delicious food made out of fresh produce grown in these fields.

reviews.author: Morris Shaxon
reviews.text: A must Visit in Hoi an is Tra Que Gardens especially the Hidden gem the jewel in the crown of Gardens. Tala or Balance. An absolute special.place .

reviews.author: Barbara Schmidt
reviews.text: Fun to ride though on a bike and see the locals growing their own food. Some good cafees around here too

reviews.author: Sam Chapman
reviews.text: Lovely place to visit and cycle walk around. There is a brilliant organic restaurant there too. Well worth a visit and doing a tour.
